name: Diachronica LLM Evolution Timeline
description: Dark-mode palette for the LLM Evolution Timeline page on diachronica.com. Extends the existing Cream & Ink dark theme with temporal progression and categorical project-type encoding.

sequential_temporal.description: A 7-stop hue-shifting scale representing 2023→2026. Direction: cool blue-gray (uncertain beginnings) through sage green (emergent systems) to amber-sienna (production heat). Avoids red-green confusion by anchoring in blue-teal-amber. Perceptually, luminosity also rises from 2023→2026, encoding progress as literal brightness.
sequential_temporal.colorblind_note: Safe for deuteranopia and protanopia: the cool-to-warm shift is distinguishable by hue angle alone, and redundant luminance gradient provides shape in simulation. Tritanopia (blue confusion) users will read this as a light-to-dark sequence, which still correctly encodes early→late.

stops.name: Slate
stops.note: Early experiments - uncertain, exploratory. Blue-gray fog.

stops.name: Patina
stops.note: Systems starting to connect - teal inflection.

stops.name: Sage
stops.note: Agent systems emerging - organic networks growing.

categorical_project_types.description: 5 hues for nominal project-type encoding. Chosen for maximum perceptual distance on dark backgrounds. Each carries appropriate associative meaning. All pass WCAG AA Large (3:1) against #1a1614; all pass AA Normal (4.5:1) as non-text UI elements.
categorical_project_types.colorblind_strategy: No two adjacent categories rely on red-green distinction alone. Blue vs teal vs purple vs gold vs amber are distinguishable under deuteranopia and protanopia via hue angle AND luminance difference. Always pair with a shape/icon redundant encoding.

rag_vector.name: Retrieval Blue
rag_vector.rationale: Information retrieval, databases, structured knowledge. Cool steel blue = precision, indexing, order. Also the 2024 temporal midpoint - anchors the RAG era in time.

agent_systems.name: Networked Teal
agent_systems.rationale: Agent networks, orchestration, multi-agent systems. Teal = living systems, neural networks, interconnection. Distinct from blue by hue, distinct from green by saturation.

clinical_accessibility.name: Lavender
clinical_accessibility.rationale: Clinical tools, accessibility work, wordblocks. Purple = care, medicine, calm focus. Sits farthest from the sienna accent on the color wheel - creates clear categorical distance.

developer_tools.name: Parchment Gold
developer_tools.rationale: CLI tools, APIs, SDKs, published packages. Gold = craft, utility, earned knowledge. Warm but distinct from sienna by hue angle. References aged documentation and technical craftsmanship.

production_infrastructure.name: Ember
production_infrastructure.rationale: Production services, service manager, Caddy, deployment. Amber-sienna = heat, load, weight, consequence. Deliberately echoes the site's existing sienna accent to signal 'this is real, this is live'.

synthetic_accent.description: The 'computation' color. Creates deliberate aesthetic tension between the organic warmth of language/linguistics (sienna, cream, parchment) and the synthetic precision of LLMs/code (terminal green-cyan). Think VS Code teal meeting a warm reading lamp.
synthetic_accent.name: Terminal Teal
synthetic_accent.rationale: This exact hue (#4EC9B0) is VS Code's default function name color. On a dark warm-brown background it reads as unmistakably synthetic - the color of electricity, computation, silicon. It pulls the eye differently than sienna does: sienna is tactile, teal is luminous. Together they encode the central thesis of the timeline: language (organic, slow) meeting computation (artificial, fast).

tension_pair.note: Use these two together on milestone cards or chapter headers to signal the human-machine encounter.

usage: Model names (GPT-4, Claude, etc.) when displayed inline

hover_glow_states.description: Hover and focus states use brightened versions of their source color plus a subtle bloom. On a very dark background, glow is cheap and atmospheric - lean into it.

glow_warm.usage: Hover on sienna-accent elements, production-infra cards, temporal 2026 nodes

glow_synth.usage: Hover on agent-system cards, synthetic-accent elements, active timeline node

glow_gold.usage: Hover on developer-tool cards, parchment-gold category items

glow_steel.usage: Hover on RAG/vector cards, early timeline nodes (2023-2024)

focus_ring.note: All keyboard focus rings: 2px solid #4EC9B0 with 2px offset. The synthetic accent is perceptually bright enough to be unmistakable on the dark bg.

production.note: Live in production - synthetic teal, electric

results.name: denim (2024/RAG)
results.note: Use at 18px+ or as non-text UI only

results.name: slate (2023)
results.note: Use for decorative/graphic only - never for text labels. Pair with cream text on top.

results.name: steel (2023.5)
results.note: Graphic use only. Label with cream text.

contrast_verification.note: 2023-era sequential stops (slate, steel) are intentionally dim - encoding uncertainty and early-stage work. Never use them as text color. Apply them as node/bar fill with cream text labels on top.

css_custom_properties.file_suggestion: Append to body.dark-mode block in /home/coolhand/servers/diachronica/static/research/css/timeline.css or a new evolution-timeline.css
